steps.expected: Both @totus/web and @totus/cli typecheck pass with exit 0
steps.observed: @totus/cli typecheck: Exited with code 0, @totus/web typecheck: Exited with code 0. Process exited with code 0.

evidence.terminal: bun --filter '*' typecheck → @totus/cli typecheck: Exited with code 0, @totus/web typecheck: Exited with code 0

steps.action: Perform CLI action: `shares list` with full-scope API key
steps.expected: Command succeeds and generates audit events
steps.observed: shares list returned 4 shares successfully (exit 0), key.used audit events generated

steps.action: Perform MCP action: `get_profile` via MCP server stdin/stdout
steps.expected: MCP server responds with profile data and generates audit events
steps.observed: MCP server returned profile with name 'test', 1 connected source, 2 active shares. key.used audit event generated.

steps.action: Browse web dashboard (login as test@totus.dev, view /dashboard)
steps.expected: Dashboard loads and generates data.viewed audit events
steps.observed: Dashboard loaded with charts showing Oura Ring data. Web-session events appear in audit log with 'YOU' badge.

steps.action: Check audit log via CLI: `audit list --output json`
steps.observed: Audit log contains 50 events including key.used (CLI/MCP), data.viewed (web/API), share.revoked (MCP), sync events. Events from all three surfaces present.

steps.action: Check audit log via web: navigate to /dashboard/audit
steps.expected: Audit page shows events from all surfaces
steps.observed: Activity Log page shows events with 'YOU' badge (web session), and 'key used' events (CLI/MCP API key). 'Synced health data from Oura', 'Viewed 3 metric(s)', and 'key used' events all visible.

evidence.network: CLI shares list → 200, MCP get_profile → success, web /dashboard → 200
evidence.terminal: CLI audit list returned 50 events with actor_type api_key and user. MCP get_profile returned profile data.

assertions.title: Read-only API key enforced across CLI

steps.action: Run `metrics list` with read-only API key (scopes: health:read, connections:read, audit:read, keys:read)
steps.observed: Command returned {"metrics": []} with exit code 0. Empty because read-only user has no health data, but the command succeeded (not blocked by scope enforcement).

steps.action: Run `shares create --label 'Test' --metrics sleep_score --start 2026-01-01 --end 2026-01-31 --expires 30` with read-only API key
steps.expected: Command fails with exit code 3 (permission error)
steps.observed: Error: 'Insufficient permissions: API key does not have the required scope: shares:write. Create a new key with the required scope.' Exit code: 3

evidence.terminal: metrics list exit 0, shares create exit 3 with scope error message

assertions.title: API key actor in audit log

steps.action: Perform actions via API key (CLI shares list, MCP get_profile, CLI audit list)
steps.observed: Multiple key.used and data.viewed events created with actor_type='api_key' and actor_id='mock_test_totus_dev'

steps.action: Check audit log via CLI with --output json
steps.expected: Events show actor_type='api_key' and key identifier
steps.observed: 42 out of 50 events have actor_type='api_key'. Each key.used event has resource_detail with api_key_id (masked). actor_id shows user ID.

steps.action: Check audit log via web at /dashboard/audit, expand a 'key used' event
steps.expected: API key events distinguishable from session events, key name/ID visible
steps.observed: API key events show no 'YOU' badge (distinguishing them from session events). Expanded detail shows api_key_id UUID (7a391890-e0b0-49af-9a3b-9ef064d5fa29) matching key 'cli-test-seed-full' (short_token: Oxcn5bC8).

evidence.terminal: CLI audit list JSON shows actor_type='api_key' on 42/50 events. Keys list shows key name 'cli-test-seed-full' maps to the api_key_id in audit.

assertions.issues: Key name not shown directly in audit event detail on web — only the api_key_id UUID is displayed in expanded view. The key name can be correlated via the keys list. CLI JSON also only shows masked api_key_id, not the key name.

steps.action: Trigger sync via CLI: `connections sync --all` with full-scope API key
Navigate to web dashboard at http://localhost:3000/dashboard
steps.expected: Dashboard shows synced data in charts
steps.observed: Dashboard loaded with Oura Ring connection shown (green dot), metric pills for Sleep Score, HRV, Resting Heart Rate, Steps, etc. Charts displaying time-series data for Sleep Score, Heart Rate Variability, and Resting Heart Rate from Oura Ring source.

evidence.terminal: connections sync --all returned queued status for oura

frictions.description: Audit log on web shows raw JSON in expanded details (e.g., {"api_key_id":"UUID"}) rather than a human-friendly display with the key name. Users must cross-reference with the keys list to identify which key was used.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 5 passed. VAL-CROSS-006 confirms both workspaces typecheck cleanly. VAL-CROSS-007 confirms audit events from CLI, MCP, and web all appear in the audit log. VAL-CROSS-008 confirms read-only key allows metrics list but blocks shares create with exit 3. VAL-CROSS-009 confirms actor_type='api_key' visible in both CLI and web audit, with key ID shown (though key name requires cross-reference). VAL-CROSS-010 confirms CLI sync triggers Oura data sync and dashboard displays the data in charts.
